message:
feat(analytics): add per-message token extraction from raw agent data

Add 440-line token extraction subsystem to the connectors module that
parses token usage data from raw message payloads across all supported
AI coding agent formats:

Token data structures:
- ExtractedTokenUsage: per-message token counts (input, output, cache_read,
  cache_creation, thinking), model identification, tool call tracking, and
  data quality indicator (API-sourced vs estimated)
- TokenDataSource enum: distinguishes actual API token counts from
  character-count-based estimates (~4 chars/token heuristic)
- ModelInfo: normalized model family/tier classification with display_name

Extraction functions per agent format:
- Claude Code: extracts from usage.input_tokens/output_tokens/cache_read/
  cache_creation_input_tokens, model from message metadata
- Codex/GPT: extracts from usage block with completion_tokens/prompt_tokens,
  detects tool_calls array for tool call counting
- Gemini: extracts from usageMetadata.promptTokenCount/candidatesTokenCount/
  cachedContentTokenCount
- Cursor: extracts from usage block similar to OpenAI format
- Generic fallback: character-count estimation for agents without token data

Model classification:
- normalize_model_info() maps model name strings to family (claude-opus,
  gpt-4o, gemini-2-pro, etc.) and tier (frontier/standard/fast/unknown)
- Handles versioned model names with pattern matching

All functions are pure (no I/O), operating on serde_json::Value inputs,
making them straightforward to unit test and compose with the schema v10
token_usage table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
